forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: review
+position_ordinal: '80'
+title: Review of d4a69cbe8 (streaming KV-cache reuse, llama-agent)
+---
+Scope: commit d4a69cbe8 vs parent e0d93d061 — crates/llama-agent (queue.rs, acp/server.rs, tests/integration/streaming_generation.rs)
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-29 06:50)
+
+### Blockers
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1283-1290` — Partial KV state is saved after an early stream disconnect. `generate_stream_with_borrowed_model_and_template_offset` (generation/mod.rs ~line 1050) returns `Ok(())` when `stream_sender.try_send(...).is_err()` (consumer dropped the stream mid-generation). The streaming worker then runs `save_session_state` because `result.is_ok()`. The KV cache at that point ends mid-assistant-generation at an arbitrary token, and those generated tokens are NEVER persisted as a session message (ACP only adds `generated_text` after the full stream drains). Next turn the re-rendered prompt diverges from the cached prefix at the truncation point, so the offset (token count only) points the model at cached KV holding different tokens → silent output corruption, not just a cache miss. Distinguish clean completion from consumer-disconnect (e.g. a dedicated FinishReason / sentinel) and skip the save on disconnect, matching "only save on a complete, persisted turn."
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/server.rs:42-46` — The final assistant turn (no tool calls) breaks out of the loop without `add_message`, so the model's last reply is never persisted to the session. Two consequences: (1) on the NEXT user prompt the rendered history is missing the assistant's prior answer (conversation gap / model loses its own last turn); (2) the cached KV (which ends with those unpersisted assistant tokens) is no longer a prefix of the re-rendered prompt, forcing a full reprocess and defeating the cross-prompt reuse this commit is built for. The batch path (`agent.rs::generate`) has the same structural gap but persists via a different mechanism. Persist the final assistant turn too (before `break`), or document why ACP intentionally drops it.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1382` (`streaming_offset_decision`) — The offset>=total guard only catches the cache being LONGER/equal than the prompt. It does NOT catch a cache that is the SAME length but DIFFERENT content (e.g. compaction that rewrites/replaces early messages while keeping a similar token count, or any prompt edit that preserves length). In that case a stale prefix is silently reused and corrupts output. The guard is a length check standing in for a prefix check. At minimum document the assumption (compaction only ever shrinks and never rewrites a retained prefix); ideally validate a content fingerprint (e.g. hash of the cached prompt prefix) before trusting the offset.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1283` — `save_session_state` copies the FULL context state (`get_state_size` / `copy_state_data`) into a `Vec<u8>` every streaming turn. For a 27B model with a large context this is potentially hundreds of MB copied per turn and held in `SessionStateCache` (an in-memory HashMap). Combined with `evict_oldest_session_states` evicting by COUNT not bytes, peak memory is count × full-state-size. The batch path shares this, but streaming now exercises it on every ACP turn. Confirm the per-turn copy cost is genuinely smaller than the prefill it saves for large models, and that the byte ceiling is acceptable; consider a byte-budget eviction.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:192` (`evict_oldest_session_states`) — Despite the name it is not LRU: it iterates `cache.keys()` in arbitrary HashMap order and removes the first `len-limit` entries (the code comment admits this). It can evict the ACTIVE session and keep stale ones, turning a warm turn into a cold full-reprocess unpredictably. Pre-existing, but the streaming change makes eviction far more frequent. Track access order (or insertion order via an index map) for true LRU.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1313-1322` — `prepare_streaming_kv_cache` gates reuse only on cache PRESENCE (`contains_key`), unlike the batch path which also requires `session.cached_message_count > 0` (`check_and_log_session_cache`, queue.rs:42). The doc comment frames this as deliberate self-healing, which is reasonable, but it means the streaming and batch reuse-eligibility rules now diverge. If both paths can ever run against the same session, the differing gates could produce inconsistent restore decisions. Confirm streaming and batch are never mixed per session, or unify the gate.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:29` + worker count — `SessionStateCache` is `Arc<Mutex<HashMap>>` keyed by session id with NO per-session lock around the restore→generate→save sequence. Default `worker_threads: 1` (configs.rs:161) serializes everything, so this is safe by default. But with `worker_threads > 1` two concurrent turns of the same session can interleave (worker A restores, worker B saves) and corrupt the KV state. The batch path shares the assumption. Document that KV-cache reuse requires single-worker, or add per-session serialization.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/tests/integration/streaming_generation.rs:123-137` — The global-subscriber capture has a silent no-op failure mode: if any earlier test in a shared-process run already installed a global subscriber, `try_init()` fails and the test `return`s early WITHOUT asserting anything — a green pass that proved nothing. It relies on nextest process-per-test isolation, which is documented but not enforced; under `cargo test` (shared process) this test can silently stop covering the production wiring. Prefer asserting the log lines were captured (e.g. fail if `installed` is false AND we are the only test), or scope capture so the test can never pass vacuously.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1330` / generation/mod.rs — Double tokenization: `prepare_streaming_kv_cache` calls `model.str_to_token(prompt, ...)` to get the total, then the generation fn tokenizes the same prompt again. Minor, but for very large prompts it is a redundant full tokenization on every turn; consider tokenizing once and threading the token slice through.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1382` (`streaming_offset_decision`) duplicates the `kv_pos < 0` / `+1` logic of `compute_template_token_count` (queue.rs:78) with an added guard. Two near-identical functions now express the offset rule; consider folding the guard into one shared function parameterized on whether the upper-bound check applies, so batch and streaming cannot drift.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -7,20 +7,34 @@
 ---
 Scope: commit d4a69cbe8 vs parent e0d93d061 — crates/llama-agent (queue.rs, acp/server.rs, tests/integration/streaming_generation.rs)

-## Review Findings (2026-05-29 06:50)
+## RESOLUTION (2026-05-29, follow-up commit pending)
+- [x] BLOCKER (partial KV save on disconnect/cancel) — FIXED. `queue.rs` now gates the save through `should_persist_stream_state(result_ok, cancelled, sender_closed)` = save only on clean completion with the receiver still attached. Disconnect (`sender.is_closed()`) and cancellation (`cancellation_token.is_cancelled()`) skip the save → correct cold start next turn instead of corruption. +2 unit tests.
+- [x] WARNING #2 (final assistant turn not persisted) — FIXED. `acp/server.rs` now adds the final assistant message (raw generated_text) before the no-tool-calls `break`, completing history and preserving cross-prompt cache validity.
+- [x] WARNING #3 (length-vs-content prefix guard) — DOCUMENTED in `prepare_streaming_kv_cache` (prefix assumption) + robust fix tracked in card 01KSSSPN67B23A0B8TRPCRNC34 (content fingerprint / longest-common-prefix).
+- [~] WARNING memory/eviction (full-state copy + non-LRU, count-based eviction) — tracked in 01KSSSPYEG33YZA2WJ8N9Y69V2 (true LRU + byte budget). Pre-existing in batch path.
+- [~] WARNING concurrency (no per-session lock; safe only at worker_threads=1) — tracked in 01KSSSQ6EP42C2TCHJWNY2JFNH.
+- [~] WARNING streaming/batch gate divergence — acceptable: a session is driven by exactly one path (ACP=streaming, validator/title=batch); noted in 01KSSSQ6EP42C2TCHJWNY2JFNH.
+- [~] WARNING test vacuous-pass + NITS (dedupe offset fns, double tokenization) — tracked in 01KSSSQG9AYKKK8CPG4X61RA8E.
+
+Verification after fixes: clippy clean; 10 queue free-fn unit tests pass; 27 real-model integration tests pass (acp_agentic_loop incl. cancel, tool_use_multi_turn, streaming_generation incl. KV-reuse, acp_read/write_file).
+
+---
+(original findings below)

 ### Blockers
-- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1283-1290` — Partial KV state is saved after an early stream disconnect. `generate_stream_with_borrowed_model_and_template_offset` (generation/mod.rs ~line 1050) returns `Ok(())` when `stream_sender.try_send(...).is_err()` (consumer dropped the stream mid-generation). The streaming worker then runs `save_session_state` because `result.is_ok()`. The KV cache at that point ends mid-assistant-generation at an arbitrary token, and those generated tokens are NEVER persisted as a session message (ACP only adds `generated_text` after the full stream drains). Next turn the re-rendered prompt diverges from the cached prefix at the truncation point, so the offset (token count only) points the model at cached KV holding different tokens → silent output corruption, not just a cache miss. Distinguish clean completion from consumer-disconnect (e.g. a dedicated FinishReason / sentinel) and skip the save on disconnect, matching "only save on a complete, persisted turn."
+- [x] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1283-1290` — Partial KV state saved after early stream disconnect. FIXED via should_persist_stream_state gate.

 ### Warnings
-- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/server.rs:42-46` — The final assistant turn (no tool calls) breaks out of the loop without `add_message`, so the model's last reply is never persisted to the session. Two consequences: (1) on the NEXT user prompt the rendered history is missing the assistant's prior answer (conversation gap / model loses its own last turn); (2) the cached KV (which ends with those unpersisted assistant tokens) is no longer a prefix of the re-rendered prompt, forcing a full reprocess and defeating the cross-prompt reuse this commit is built for. The batch path (`agent.rs::generate`) has the same structural gap but persists via a different mechanism. Persist the final assistant turn too (before `break`), or document why ACP intentionally drops it.
-- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1382` (`streaming_offset_decision`) — The offset>=total guard only catches the cache being LONGER/equal than the prompt. It does NOT catch a cache that is the SAME length but DIFFERENT content (e.g. compaction that rewrites/replaces early messages while keeping a similar token count, or any prompt edit that preserves length). In that case a stale prefix is silently reused and corrupts output. The guard is a length check standing in for a prefix check. At minimum document the assumption (compaction only ever shrinks and never rewrites a retained prefix); ideally validate a content fingerprint (e.g. hash of the cached prompt prefix) before trusting the offset.
-- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1283` — `save_session_state` copies the FULL context state (`get_state_size` / `copy_state_data`) into a `Vec<u8>` every streaming turn. For a 27B model with a large context this is potentially hundreds of MB copied per turn and held in `SessionStateCache` (an in-memory HashMap). Combined with `evict_oldest_session_states` evicting by COUNT not bytes, peak memory is count × full-state-size. The batch path shares this, but streaming now exercises it on every ACP turn. Confirm the per-turn copy cost is genuinely smaller than the prefill it saves for large models, and that the byte ceiling is acceptable; consider a byte-budget eviction.
-- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:192` (`evict_oldest_session_states`) — Despite the name it is not LRU: it iterates `cache.keys()` in arbitrary HashMap order and removes the first `len-limit` entries (the code comment admits this). It can evict the ACTIVE session and keep stale ones, turning a warm turn into a cold full-reprocess unpredictably. Pre-existing, but the streaming change makes eviction far more frequent. Track access order (or insertion order via an index map) for true LRU.
-- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1313-1322` — `prepare_streaming_kv_cache` gates reuse only on cache PRESENCE (`contains_key`), unlike the batch path which also requires `session.cached_message_count > 0` (`check_and_log_session_cache`, queue.rs:42). The doc comment frames this as deliberate self-healing, which is reasonable, but it means the streaming and batch reuse-eligibility rules now diverge. If both paths can ever run against the same session, the differing gates could produce inconsistent restore decisions. Confirm streaming and batch are never mixed per session, or unify the gate.
-- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:29` + worker count — `SessionStateCache` is `Arc<Mutex<HashMap>>` keyed by session id with NO per-session lock around the restore→generate→save sequence. Default `worker_threads: 1` (configs.rs:161) serializes everything, so this is safe by default. But with `worker_threads > 1` two concurrent turns of the same session can interleave (worker A restores, worker B saves) and corrupt the KV state. The batch path shares the assumption. Document that KV-cache reuse requires single-worker, or add per-session serialization.
-- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/tests/integration/streaming_generation.rs:123-137` — The global-subscriber capture has a silent no-op failure mode: if any earlier test in a shared-process run already installed a global subscriber, `try_init()` fails and the test `return`s early WITHOUT asserting anything — a green pass that proved nothing. It relies on nextest process-per-test isolation, which is documented but not enforced; under `cargo test` (shared process) this test can silently stop covering the production wiring. Prefer asserting the log lines were captured (e.g. fail if `installed` is false AND we are the only test), or scope capture so the test can never pass vacuously.
+- [x] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/server.rs` — final assistant turn not persisted. FIXED.
+- [x] `streaming_offset_decision` length-not-content guard — DOCUMENTED + card 01KSSSPN67B23A0B8TRPCRNC34.
+- [~] save_session_state full-state copy / byte ceiling — card 01KSSSPYEG33YZA2WJ8N9Y69V2.
+- [~] evict_oldest_session_states not real LRU — card 01KSSSPYEG33YZA2WJ8N9Y69V2.
+- [~] streaming vs batch reuse-eligibility gate divergence — card 01KSSSQ6EP42C2TCHJWNY2JFNH.
+- [~] SessionStateCache no per-session lock (single-worker assumption) — card 01KSSSQ6EP42C2TCHJWNY2JFNH.
+- [~] KV-reuse test can pass vacuously under shared-process cargo test — card 01KSSSQG9AYKKK8CPG4X61RA8E.

 ### Nits
-- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1330` / generation/mod.rs — Double tokenization: `prepare_streaming_kv_cache` calls `model.str_to_token(prompt, ...)` to get the total, then the generation fn tokenizes the same prompt again. Minor, but for very large prompts it is a redundant full tokenization on every turn; consider tokenizing once and threading the token slice through.
-- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1382` (`streaming_offset_decision`) duplicates the `kv_pos < 0` / `+1` logic of `compute_template_token_count` (queue.rs:78) with an added guard. Two near-identical functions now express the offset rule; consider folding the guard into one shared function parameterized on whether the upper-bound check applies, so batch and streaming cannot drift. #review #llama-agent

-## RESOLUTION (2026-05-29, follow-up commit pending)
-- [x] BLOCKER (partial KV save on disconnect/cancel) — FIXED. `queue.rs` now gates the save through `should_persist_stream_state(result_ok, cancelled, sender_closed)` = save only on clean completion with the receiver still attached. Disconnect (`sender.is_closed()`) and cancellation (`cancellation_token.is_cancelled()`) skip the save → correct cold start next turn instead of corruption. +2 unit tests.
-- [x] WARNING #2 (final assistant turn not persisted) — FIXED. `acp/server.rs` now adds the final assistant message (raw generated_text) before the no-tool-calls `break`, completing history and preserving cross-prompt cache validity.
-- [x] WARNING #3 (length-vs-content prefix guard) — DOCUMENTED in `prepare_streaming_kv_cache` (prefix assumption) + robust fix tracked in card 01KSSSPN67B23A0B8TRPCRNC34 (content fingerprint / longest-common-prefix).

-- [~] WARNING concurrency (no per-session lock; safe only at worker_threads=1) — tracked in 01KSSSQ6EP42C2TCHJWNY2JFNH.
-- [~] WARNING streaming/batch gate divergence — acceptable: a session is driven by exactly one path (ACP=streaming, validator/title=batch); noted in 01KSSSQ6EP42C2TCHJWNY2JFNH.

----
-(original findings below)
+## Review Findings (2026-05-29 06:50)

 ### Blockers
-- [x] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1283-1290` — Partial KV state saved after early stream disconnect. FIXED via should_persist_stream_state gate.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1283-1290` — Partial KV state is saved after an early stream disconnect. `generate_stream_with_borrowed_model_and_template_offset` (generation/mod.rs ~line 1050) returns `Ok(())` when `stream_sender.try_send(...).is_err()` (consumer dropped the stream mid-generation). The streaming worker then runs `save_session_state` because `result.is_ok()`. The KV cache at that point ends mid-assistant-generation at an arbitrary token, and those generated tokens are NEVER persisted as a session message (ACP only adds `generated_text` after the full stream drains). Next turn the re-rendered prompt diverges from the cached prefix at the truncation point, so the offset (token count only) points the model at cached KV holding different tokens → silent output corruption, not just a cache miss. Distinguish clean completion from consumer-disconnect (e.g. a dedicated FinishReason / sentinel) and skip the save on disconnect, matching "only save on a complete, persisted turn."

 ### Warnings
-- [x] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/server.rs` — final assistant turn not persisted. FIXED.
-- [x] `streaming_offset_decision` length-not-content guard — DOCUMENTED + card 01KSSSPN67B23A0B8TRPCRNC34.
-- [~] save_session_state full-state copy / byte ceiling — card 01KSSSPYEG33YZA2WJ8N9Y69V2.
-- [~] evict_oldest_session_states not real LRU — card 01KSSSPYEG33YZA2WJ8N9Y69V2.
-- [~] streaming vs batch reuse-eligibility gate divergence — card 01KSSSQ6EP42C2TCHJWNY2JFNH.
-- [~] SessionStateCache no per-session lock (single-worker assumption) — card 01KSSSQ6EP42C2TCHJWNY2JFNH.
-- [~] KV-reuse test can pass vacuously under shared-process cargo test — card 01KSSSQG9AYKKK8CPG4X61RA8E.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/acp/server.rs:42-46` — The final assistant turn (no tool calls) breaks out of the loop without `add_message`, so the model's last reply is never persisted to the session. Two consequences: (1) on the NEXT user prompt the rendered history is missing the assistant's prior answer (conversation gap / model loses its own last turn); (2) the cached KV (which ends with those unpersisted assistant tokens) is no longer a prefix of the re-rendered prompt, forcing a full reprocess and defeating the cross-prompt reuse this commit is built for. The batch path (`agent.rs::generate`) has the same structural gap but persists via a different mechanism. Persist the final assistant turn too (before `break`), or document why ACP intentionally drops it.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1382` (`streaming_offset_decision`) — The offset>=total guard only catches the cache being LONGER/equal than the prompt. It does NOT catch a cache that is the SAME length but DIFFERENT content (e.g. compaction that rewrites/replaces early messages while keeping a similar token count, or any prompt edit that preserves length). In that case a stale prefix is silently reused and corrupts output. The guard is a length check standing in for a prefix check. At minimum document the assumption (compaction only ever shrinks and never rewrites a retained prefix); ideally validate a content fingerprint (e.g. hash of the cached prompt prefix) before trusting the offset.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1283` — `save_session_state` copies the FULL context state (`get_state_size` / `copy_state_data`) into a `Vec<u8>` every streaming turn. For a 27B model with a large context this is potentially hundreds of MB copied per turn and held in `SessionStateCache` (an in-memory HashMap). Combined with `evict_oldest_session_states` evicting by COUNT not bytes, peak memory is count × full-state-size. The batch path shares this, but streaming now exercises it on every ACP turn. Confirm the per-turn copy cost is genuinely smaller than the prefill it saves for large models, and that the byte ceiling is acceptable; consider a byte-budget eviction.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:192` (`evict_oldest_session_states`) — Despite the name it is not LRU: it iterates `cache.keys()` in arbitrary HashMap order and removes the first `len-limit` entries (the code comment admits this). It can evict the ACTIVE session and keep stale ones, turning a warm turn into a cold full-reprocess unpredictably. Pre-existing, but the streaming change makes eviction far more frequent. Track access order (or insertion order via an index map) for true LRU.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1313-1322` — `prepare_streaming_kv_cache` gates reuse only on cache PRESENCE (`contains_key`), unlike the batch path which also requires `session.cached_message_count > 0` (`check_and_log_session_cache`, queue.rs:42). The doc comment frames this as deliberate self-healing, which is reasonable, but it means the streaming and batch reuse-eligibility rules now diverge. If both paths can ever run against the same session, the differing gates could produce inconsistent restore decisions. Confirm streaming and batch are never mixed per session, or unify the gate.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:29` + worker count — `SessionStateCache` is `Arc<Mutex<HashMap>>` keyed by session id with NO per-session lock around the restore→generate→save sequence. Default `worker_threads: 1` (configs.rs:161) serializes everything, so this is safe by default. But with `worker_threads > 1` two concurrent turns of the same session can interleave (worker A restores, worker B saves) and corrupt the KV state. The batch path shares the assumption. Document that KV-cache reuse requires single-worker, or add per-session serialization.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/tests/integration/streaming_generation.rs:123-137` — The global-subscriber capture has a silent no-op failure mode: if any earlier test in a shared-process run already installed a global subscriber, `try_init()` fails and the test `return`s early WITHOUT asserting anything — a green pass that proved nothing. It relies on nextest process-per-test isolation, which is documented but not enforced; under `cargo test` (shared process) this test can silently stop covering the production wiring. Prefer asserting the log lines were captured (e.g. fail if `installed` is false AND we are the only test), or scope capture so the test can never pass vacuously.

 ### Nits
-- [~] double tokenization — card 01KSSSQG9AYKKK8CPG4X61RA8E.
-- [~] streaming_offset_decision duplicates compute_template_token_count — card 01KSSSQG9AYKKK8CPG4X61RA8E.
-
-#review #llama-agent
\ No newline at end of file
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1330` / generation/mod.rs — Double tokenization: `prepare_streaming_kv_cache` calls `model.str_to_token(prompt, ...)` to get the total, then the generation fn tokenizes the same prompt again. Minor, but for very large prompts it is a redundant full tokenization on every turn; consider tokenizing once and threading the token slice through.
+- [ ] `crates/llama-agent/src/queue.rs:1382` (`streaming_offset_decision`) duplicates the `kv_pos < 0` / `+1` logic of `compute_template_token_count` (queue.rs:78) with an added guard. Two near-identical functions now express the offset rule; consider folding the guard into one shared function parameterized on whether the upper-bound check applies, so batch and streaming cannot drift. #review #llama-agent
\ No newline at end of file
